member.name: T:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OpeColumnSizing
member.summary: Provides column size calculations for OPE-encoded values. Use these to configure database column lengths (e.g., nvarchar(N)).

member.name: F:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OpeColumnSizing.CodeWidth
member.summary: Width of each encoded character token (6 digits).

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OpeColumnSizing.EncodedStringLength(System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculates the encoded string length for a given maximum plaintext length. Example: MaxLength=100 → EncodedLength=600.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OpeColumnSizing.EncodedIntegerLength(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Calculates the encoded integer column length.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OpeColumnSizing.EncodedDecimalLength(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Calculates the encoded decimal column length.

member.name: T:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder
member.summary:
Order-Preserving Encoder (OPE)
Every character in the supported universe maps to a fixed-width numeric code. Codes are assigned in strict ascending order — smaller input always produces a smaller code, guaranteeing database-level ORDER BY, range queries, and prefix LIKE queries work correctly on the encoded ciphertext without decryption.
A client-supplied key derives a deterministic visual offset that shifts all codes by a key-specific amount.

member.name: F:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.CODE_WIDTH
member.summary: Width of each encoded token in decimal digits.

member.name: F:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.DEFAULT_NUMBER_PAD_WIDTH
member.summary: Default maximum digit width for numeric values (supports up to 999,999,999,999).

member.name: P:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.SupportsNegatives
member.summary: Whether this encoder supports negative numbers.

member.name: P:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.NumberPadWidth
member.summary: The configured number pad width.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32,System.Boolean,SecureORM.Core.Normalization.IInputNormalizer)
member.summary: Initialise the encoder for a specific client.
member.param: A secret string unique to this client/tenant.
Maximum digit width for numeric columns (1-18).
When true, negative integers and decimals are supported via a sign-prefix scheme. Encoded data is NOT compatible between supportNegatives=true and false.
Optional input normalizer for Unicode handling.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.EncodeString(System.String)
member.summary: Encodes a string value. The resulting ciphertext sorts lexicographically in the same order as the original plaintext.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.DecodeString(System.String)
member.summary: Decodes a string ciphertext back to its plaintext.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.EncodeInteger(System.Int64)
member.summary: Encodes an integer value. When supportNegatives is enabled, negative values use a sign-prefix + nines' complement scheme to preserve sort order.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.DecodeInteger(System.String)
member.summary: Decodes an integer ciphertext back to its long value.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.EncodeDecimal(System.Decimal,System.Int32)
member.summary: Encodes a decimal/float value. When supportNegatives is enabled, negative values are supported with preserved sort order.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.DecodeDecimal(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Decodes a decimal ciphertext.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.EncodePrefix(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the encoded prefix for a SQL LIKE '{prefix}%' query.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.EncodeStringRange(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns encoded bounds for a SQL BETWEEN query on a string column.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.EncodeIntegerRange(System.Int64,System.Int64)
member.summary: Returns encoded bounds for a SQL BETWEEN query on an integer column.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.EncodeDecimalRange(System.Decimal,System.Decimal,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns encoded bounds for a SQL BETWEEN query on a decimal column.

member.name: M:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.GetEncodingTableUnsafe
member.summary: Returns the full encoding table for inspection / debugging.

member.name: P:SecureORM.Core.Encoding.OPEEncoder.UniverseSize
member.summary: Returns the number of characters in the supported universe.

member.name: T:SecureORM.Core.Normalization.IInputNormalizer
member.summary: Normalizes input strings before OPE encoding. Implement this interface to handle Unicode, accented characters, or custom transformations. Note: Decoding returns the normalized form, not the original input.

member.name: T:SecureORM.Core.Normalization.UnicodeNormalizer
member.summary: Normalizes Unicode input to ASCII-safe strings suitable for OPE encoding. Applies NFC normalization, optional transliteration, and optional lowercasing.

member.name: P:SecureORM.Core.Normalization.UnicodeNormalizerOptions.NormalizationForm
member.summary: Unicode normalization form (default: NFC).

member.name: P:SecureORM.Core.Normalization.UnicodeNormalizerOptions.Transliterate
member.summary: When true, transliterate accented characters to ASCII equivalents.

member.name: P:SecureORM.Core.Normalization.UnicodeNormalizerOptions.ReplacementChar
member.summary: Replacement character for characters that cannot be transliterated. Set to null to throw on untransliterable characters.

member.name: P:SecureORM.Core.Normalization.UnicodeNormalizerOptions.ToLowerCase
member.summary: When true, convert all characters to lowercase before encoding.
